source: Step 5 persona revisit synthetic UX inspection

items.title: Investigate cross-route browser errors during persona revisits
evidence: Step 5 passed for 17 selected personas with 0 hard test failures, but issue candidates recorded browser errors on every core route.

Observed counts repeated across sampled reports: / and /home reported consoleErrors=30, /discover reported consoleErrors=45, /profile reported consoleErrors=27, /watchlist and /history reported consoleErrors=21.
Aggregate issue severity summary contained 136 P2 synthetic issue candidates, dominated by browser error evidence and trust-trigger activation.
items.whyItMatters: Persona trust and clarity scores cannot be interpreted confidently while every core route reports browser-level errors.
items.suggestedNextAction: Run a focused local Playwright trace for one persona, group browser errors by route and stack, and fix the shared root cause before product-tuning recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: A P11 smoke revisit records zero browser errors on /, /home, /discover, /profile, /watchlist, and /history.
An all-persona revisit records no cross-route browser-error issue candidates for the six core routes.
The safe revisit summary clearly distinguishes route visit success from browser diagnostic warnings.
nonGoals: Do not change recommendation ranking or taste scoring.
Do not alter visible UX, copy, layout, or styling as part of this investigation.
Do not inspect raw session state or generated browser artifacts.

items.title: Fix or explain thin/error behavior on the Cinematic DNA profile route
evidence: Every sampled report recorded recovery or error copy on /profile.
Every sampled report recorded /profile as empty or thin for the persona.
The aggregate weakest surface list ranked /profile with 17 visits, 17 issues, and 17 trust signals.
items.whyItMatters: Profile is where personas expect proof that onboarding and viewing history compound into Cinematic DNA. A thin or errored profile blocks trust for evidence-seeking and anti-slop personas.
items.suggestedNextAction: Debug the /profile data-loading path with a freshly onboarded persona and determine whether the route is missing required rows, querying the wrong shape, or classifying a load failure as an empty profile.
acceptanceCriteria: /profile loads without recovery/error copy for P1, P2, P5, P11, and P14 after local onboarding.
The route shows either a credible Cinematic DNA state or an explicit honest empty state that is not caused by a failed data load.
A persona revisit no longer flags /profile as empty or thin for all 17 personas.
nonGoals: Do not redesign the profile page.
Do not rewrite Cinematic DNA copy.
Do not seed taste data directly as a shortcut for the browser workflow.

items.title: Clarify watchlist and history recovery states after onboarding
evidence: Sampled reports recorded recovery or error copy on /watchlist and /history.
P5's survival instinct depends on memory and no-repeat behavior, but the history surface showed route-level errors.
P11 and P14 reports treated thin/error states as reasons the product felt less ready than onboarding implied.
items.whyItMatters: Watchlist and history are the proof surfaces for saved and watched memory. If they look broken after onboarding, personas cannot trust that future recommendations will compound.
items.suggestedNextAction: Trace /watchlist and /history with a local persona, classify whether the state is a real empty list or a failed load, and fix any query or data-shape mismatch.
acceptanceCriteria: /watchlist and /history load without recovery/error copy for at least P5 and P11.
The revisit summary records a clear empty state only when the underlying data truly has no rows.
P5's report no longer treats history memory as blocked by route errors.
nonGoals: Do not add new watchlist or history features.
Do not alter recommendation de-duplication behavior.
Do not use direct database seeding to make the route appear populated.

items.title: Make a movie detail path reachable from Home or Discover during persona revisit
evidence: Every sampled report recorded /movie/:id as not_found.
The revisit runner could not find a safe /movie/:id link from /home or /discover.
Personas still saw recommendation or pick language, but could not reach detail evidence through the captured path.
items.whyItMatters: Movie detail is where evidence, metadata, availability, trailer energy, and Film File trust can be inspected. If the revisit cannot reach it, believability remains shallow.
items.suggestedNextAction: Confirm whether Home and Discover expose a real link to a selected film in the current UI and, if they do, update the runner's safe locator. If they do not, file a scoped product task for a reachable detail path.
acceptanceCriteria: A P11 revisit captures one visited /movie/:id observation from either /home or /discover.
The captured detail route includes a safe heading or title for the selected film.
The runner still records not_found without failing when no detail link is available.
nonGoals: Do not change recommendation selection.
Do not add new metadata providers.
Do not evaluate where-to-watch or trailer depth until the detail route is reliably reachable.

items.title: Separate true empty states from failed load states in revisit findings
evidence: The current revisit artifacts report empty or thin states and recovery/error copy together.
A true empty state has different product meaning than a route that failed to load personalized data.
P15 cold-start and P16 warmed-up personas need this distinction to make longitudinal comparisons credible.
items.whyItMatters: Without separating empty from failed, the backlog can overstate product gaps or miss technical failures that invalidate persona interpretation.
items.suggestedNextAction: Add route observation fields that classify empty, failed_load, and loaded_with_no_rows separately using visible copy and safe app-side signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Revisit summary includes a route-level state classification for each surface.
Reports distinguish OBSERVED true empty states from OBSERVED failed load states.
Persona voice feedback does not infer product thinness from a technical load failure.
nonGoals: Do not modify visible empty-state copy.
Do not add direct database probes to the persona revisit flow by default.
Do not store raw page snapshots in committed files.

items.title: Group persona revisit diagnostics by route and root cause
evidence: The Step 5 artifact reports 136 repeated P2 issue candidates.
Many candidates repeat the same browser-error pattern across personas and routes.
The current summary is useful, but still requires manual grouping before it becomes a fix queue.
items.whyItMatters: Repeated per-persona evidence is good for confidence, but engineers need grouped root-cause buckets to act efficiently.
items.suggestedNextAction: Enhance the revisit summary or helper summary to include grouped issue counts by route, issue title, and first safe evidence line.
acceptanceCriteria: The summary output includes grouped issue counts by route and issue.
Repeated browser-error candidates collapse into a small diagnostic table.
Per-persona detail remains available without duplicating the main backlog.
nonGoals: Do not remove per-persona reports.
Do not collect sensitive browser storage.
Do not depend on agent-specific tooling.

items.title: Make fresh local persona QA reproducible from durable schema/bootstrap
evidence: Prior local persona steps required attention to local Supabase schema and Vite environment alignment.
Step 6 should preserve a path where provisioning, auth states, onboarding, and revisit can run after a fresh local reset.
Technical route failures should not be hidden by ad hoc local scaffolding.
items.whyItMatters: Persona QA only compounds if future developers can recreate the same local baseline without private setup knowledge.
items.suggestedNextAction: Capture the authoritative base Supabase schema in migrations or a documented bootstrap workflow, then validate the full persona chain after a local reset.
acceptanceCriteria: A fresh local reset can run persona account provisioning, auth-state generation, onboarding, and revisit from documented commands.
Required local Vite environment values are documented without committing secrets.
No ad hoc schema patch is needed outside the durable workflow.
nonGoals: Do not support production persona provisioning.
Do not add production data workflows.
Do not hardcode project-specific credentials.

items.title: Re-check persona-specific trust lenses after technical blockers are fixed
evidence: Step 5 observed promising mood, DNA, watched, and saved language.
P11 needs decision relief, P14 needs anti-slop proof, P2 needs evidence and number credibility, and P5 needs memory/no-repeat confidence.
P4 where-to-watch and P9 visual/trailer energy remain product-scope-sensitive until the detail route and technical confidence blockers are resolved.
items.whyItMatters: These persona lenses are the right product questions, but current technical blockers make product tuning premature.
items.suggestedNextAction: After P1 blockers pass, rerun P11, P14, P2, P5, P4, and P9 and compare their reports before tuning recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: A follow-up revisit has zero P1 technical confidence blockers.
Persona reports include route evidence for the surfaces each trust lens depends on.
Any recommendation or product changes are based on observed post-fix evidence, not this blocked baseline alone.
nonGoals: Do not tune recommendation logic from the current blocked baseline.
Do not claim these synthetic findings are real user feedback.
Do not expand scope into availability or trailer provider integrations before detail-route confidence exists.
